subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the plot and characters in Anton Chekhov’s ‘The Proposal’
Identify the main events of the story and their sequence
Recognize the humorous elements and misunderstandings in the story
Learn about character traits such as pride, stubbornness, and humor
Develop reading comprehension and summarization skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Context
topics_covered: Introduction to Anton Chekhov and Russian literature context
Setting of the story: countryside and characters
Purpose of the play: comedy and satire
teaching_strategy: Storytelling with simple background information about the author and setting
Discussion of the story as a one-act play with humorous elements
student_activities: Locate Russia on the map and discuss rural life in the 19th century
Predict what the story might be about based on the title
assessment: Oral Q&A on context and author
Short written note on initial predictions

lesson_plan.title: Introduction of Characters
teaching_strategy: Discuss each character’s personality in simple language
student_activities: List main characters and their traits
Discuss how characters’ traits may lead to humorous situations
assessment: Short written note summarizing each character

lesson_plan.title: Key Events and Conflicts
topics_covered: Arguments and misunderstandings about property and dogs
Humorous situations created by pride and stubbornness
teaching_strategy: Use storytelling to narrate events in chronological order
Highlight humorous elements and simple conflicts
student_activities: Sequence events on a simple timeline
Identify incidents that create humor and conflict
assessment: Short written note on one humorous incident

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Humor
topics_covered: Main theme: misunderstandings, pride, and social manners
Use of humor to convey satire
Lesson on human nature and relationships
teaching_strategy: Discuss themes and humor using simple examples
Explain how misunderstandings lead to comedy
student_activities: Identify a theme and a humorous incident from the text
Discuss in pairs how humor is created through dialogue
assessment: Oral discussion on themes and humor
Short written note summarizing a lesson learned

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Reflection
topics_covered: Summary of the story and key events
Character traits and their impact on the story
teaching_strategy: Use charts, flashcards, or timelines for revision
Encourage students to share favorite incidents and reflections
student_activities: Create a simple chart with characters, traits, and key incidents
Write a few lines on a humorous incident they liked most
assessment: Oral quiz on events, characters, and humor
Class discussion on reflections and lessons learned

teaching_aids: Map showing Russia and rural settings
Images or illustrations of characters and setting
Timeline chart of events for one-act play
teacher_tip: Focus on storytelling and humor to engage students. Highlight character traits and their role in creating comic situations. Use visuals, timelines, and simple discussions for easy comprehension.
